  1. Install i18n library (react-i18next or similar)

     new Intl.DateTimeFormat(locale, options).format(date)

    [31mWhat is Image Compress?[39m
    [31m   Image Compress is a free browser-based tool that reduces image file sizes without significant quality loss. Unlike cloud-based compressors that upload your photos to remote servers, this tool processes everything locally using JavaScript. Your images stay on your device throughout compression.  Perfect for optimizing website images, reducing email attachment sizes, or saving storage space.     [39m
    [31mHow does Image Compress work?[39m
    [31m   01 Drag and drop images onto the upload area, or click to browse your files  02 Adjust the quality slider to balance file size vs image quality  03 Preview the compressed image and compare with the original  04 Click download to save your optimized image  05 Process multiple images in batch for faster workflow      [39m
    [31mWhy use a browser-based tool?[39m
    [31m      Privacy first: Images never leave your device or get uploaded to any server     Speed: No upload/download wait times—compression is instant     Security: No risk of your photos being stored or accessed by third parties     Offline capable: Works without internet after page loads     No limits: Compress as many images as you want, no daily quotas      Common Questions    How much can I reduce image file size?   Typically 60-90% reduction depending on the original image and quality settings. A 5MB photo can often be reduced to under 500KB while maintaining excellent visual quality.     Are my images uploaded to a server?   No. All compression happens entirely in your browser using JavaScript. Your images never leave your device. You can verify this by disconnecting from the internet after the page loads—the tool will still work.     What image formats are supported?   We support PNG, JPEG, and WebP formats. The tool automatically detects your image format and applies optimal compression settings.     Will compression reduce image quality?   You control the quality level. At 80% quality, most images look nearly identical to the original. The preview feature lets you compare before downloading.     Can I compress multiple images at once?   Yes. Drag and drop multiple images or select several files at once. Each image is processed individually and can be downloaded separately or as a batch.     [31mWhat is PDF Merge?[39m
    [31m   PDF Merge is a free browser-based tool that combines multiple PDF documents into a single file. Unlike traditional PDF mergers that upload your files to remote servers, this tool processes everything locally using JavaScript and the pdf-lib library. Your documents stay on your device throughout the entire process.  Ideal for combining invoices, contracts, reports, or any documents you need in a single PDF.     [39m
    [31mHow does PDF Merge work?[39m
    [31m   01 Drag and drop PDF files onto the upload area, or click to browse your files  02 Reorder files by dragging them into your preferred sequence  03 Remove any unwanted files by clicking the X button  04 Click \"Merge PDFs\" to combine all files into one document  05 Download your merged PDF instantly—no email required      [39m
    [31mWhy use a browser-based tool?[39m
    [31m      Privacy first: Files never leave your device or get uploaded to any server     Speed: No upload/download wait times—processing is instant     Security: No risk of data breaches or third-party access to your documents     Offline capable: Works without internet connection after page loads     No limits: Merge as many PDFs as you want, no daily quotas or file size caps      Common Questions    Is there a file size limit for merging PDFs?   There's no enforced file size limit. However, very large files (100MB+) may be slower to process depending on your device's memory. For best performance, we recommend keeping individual files under 50MB.     Are my PDF files uploaded to a server?   No. All processing happens entirely in your browser using JavaScript. Your files never leave your device. You can verify this by disconnecting from the internet after the page loads—the tool will still work.     Can I merge password-protected PDFs?   Currently, password-protected PDFs must be unlocked before merging. We recommend using a PDF password remover first, then merging the unlocked files.     What browsers are supported?   PDF Merge works in all modern browsers including Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge. For best performance, use the latest version of your preferred browser.     Can I reorder pages from different PDFs?   Yes. After adding your PDFs, you can drag and drop to reorder entire files. For page-level reordering, use our PDF Split tool to extract specific pages, then merge them in your desired order.     [31mWhat is Image Compress?[39m
    [31m   Image Compress is a free browser-based tool that reduces image file sizes without significant quality loss. Unlike cloud-based compressors that upload your photos to remote servers, this tool processes everything locally using JavaScript. Your images stay on your device throughout compression.  Perfect for optimizing website images, reducing email attachment sizes, or saving storage space.     [39m
    [31mHow does Image Compress work?[39m
    [31m   01 Drag and drop images onto the upload area, or click to browse your files  02 Adjust the quality slider to balance file size vs image quality  03 Preview the compressed image and compare with the original  04 Click download to save your optimized image  05 Process multiple images in batch for faster workflow      [39m
    [31mWhy use a browser-based tool?[39m
    [31m      Privacy first: Images never leave your device or get uploaded to any server     Speed: No upload/download wait times—compression is instant     Security: No risk of your photos being stored or accessed by third parties     Offline capable: Works without internet after page loads     No limits: Compress as many images as you want, no daily quotas      Common Questions    How much can I reduce image file size?   Typically 60-90% reduction depending on the original image and quality settings. A 5MB photo can often be reduced to under 500KB while maintaining excellent visual quality.     Are my images uploaded to a server?   No. All compression happens entirely in your browser using JavaScript. Your images never leave your device. You can verify this by disconnecting from the internet after the page loads—the tool will still work.     What image formats are supported?   We support PNG, JPEG, and WebP formats. The tool automatically detects your image format and applies optimal compression settings.     Will compression reduce image quality?   You control the quality level. At 80% quality, most images look nearly identical to the original. The preview feature lets you compare before downloading.     Can I compress multiple images at once?   Yes. Drag and drop multiple images or select several files at once. Each image is processed individually and can be downloaded separately or as a batch.
